Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendants Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-13-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Rebecca Coll, Esq. (415-426-3502) of Quadra & Coll LLP for Plaintiff.
Lauren Jones, Esq. (707-509-9882 ljones@kdvlaw.com) and Erica Goodwin, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich Voluck for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JEFF MOSLEY'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendants Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint is denied. Contrary to defendants' representations, no new facts or law are raised by their motion. Case number CGC-19-579925 has been pending in this court since 2019 and was discussed in both sides' demurrer papers, including the subject of plaintiff filing a cross-complaint in that case.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Rebecca Coll, Esq. (415-426-3502; rcoll@quadracoll.com) of Quadra & Coll LLP for Plaintiffs.
Shane Kolding, Esq. (skolding@kdvlaw.com) and Ashley Klein, Esq. (415-926-7600; aklein@kdvlaw.com) of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck, LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JEFF MOSLEY'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to the complaint in its entirety is sustained without leave to amend on the basis of the compulsory cross-complaint statute. Defendant's demurrer on the basis of the sham pleading doctrine is overruled.

Under Code of Civil Procedure Section 426.30, "if a party against whom a complaint has been filed and served fails to allege in a cross-complaint any related cause of action which . . . he has against the plaintiff, such party may not thereafter in any other action assert against the plaintiff the related cause of action not pleaded." "The compulsory cross-complaint statute is designed to prevent piecemeal litigation. . . . Because of the liberal construction given to the statute to accomplish its purpose of avoiding a multiplicity of actions, transaction is construed broadly; it is not confined to a single, isolated act or occurrence but may embrace a series of acts or occurrences logically interrelated. . . . We conclude that the demurrer was properly sustained because the claims as alleged were barred under section 426.30." (Align Technology, Inc. v. Tran (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 949, 995-961.)

Plaintiff's allegations here are logically related to those in Defendant's cross-complaint, in action no. 579925. There, Mr. Mosley alleges that "[o]n or around February 2009, Clarisa Durden allowed her brother Homero Rochon and his now wife Dori Rochon to occupy a downstairs portion of the property. . . . The terms of this arrangement evolved over time, and a time no later than January 2016, the arrangement was that Homero Rochon and Dori Rochon would live rent free and utilities paid in return for helping to care for Ms. Durden . . . ." (Mosley Cross-Compl., 11.) Plaintiff's claims here allege that Mr. Mosley hired her to "provide Durden with necessary care and support" and that she and her husband "moved into Durden's home in October 2008, at which time [Dori] Rochon began providing the agreed-upon services . . . ." (Compl., 6-7.) Plaintiff is granted leave to file a cross-complaint in action no. 579925.

Defendant's demurrer under the sham pleading doctrine is overruled. The sham pleading doctrine binds a plaintiff "by [earlier] allegations" and the court may properly consider those allegations when ruling on a demurrer. (Larson v. UHS of Rancho Springs, Inc. (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 336, 345.) Defendant does not show that even if the doctrine applies here to bind Plaintiff to the allegations in her answer, that Plaintiff is unable to state a claim under any of the theories in this action.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964. =(302/RBU)
